en.name: Testing New European Bauhaus Solutions for Deep Green Transformation of Cities and Regions in Euro-MED area.
en.description:
Average citizen usually considers ecology and green transformation as something that “must be” done, instead of something they would “love to” do. To change this perception, the New European Bauhaus (NEB) initiative introduced aesthetic (beautiful) aspects along with citizens participation to support urban green transformation.

Project Bauhaus4MED will test innovative NEB solutions integrating sustainability, inclusion and aesthetic aspects into decision making process, for improvement of green living areas. Results from Interreg MED BLUE CROWDFUNDING project will be upgraded into a new “CROWDVOCACY” tool combining crowdfunding, crowdsourcing and participative democracy principles, with aesthetic green architecture design resulting in citizens tailor-made solutions for better living.

Each of the 5 testing regions/cities will identify participative green community challenges, use crowdsourcing to co-create solution ideas, run civic crowdfunding campaigns to co-finance solutions and use participative budgets to implement pilot solutions. Pilot solutions will be focused on circular building materials, regenerated green urban spaces, green products, or green lifestyle. Transferrable toolbox will be offered as a solution including best example catalogue, lessons learned and CROWDVOCACY digital tool. To mainstream NEB principles into the decision making process, and ensure long term sustainability, the regions/cities will develop green transformation action plans, using green, participative, and aesthetic principles.

Project will improve policy impact, transferability and durability of outputs and deliverables by organizing significant number of regional and EU level events taking advantage of PP10 IFLA Europe unique position as member of NEB COLLECTIVE. Actions will include high-policy NEB annual conferences in Brussels, regions joining NEB initiative, Transnational Green Transformation Challenge event and preparation of Applications to NEB Rising Stars 2025.
en.expected_achievements:
Work package will test and develop solutions for integration of sustainable, inclusive and aesthetic dimensions into green transformation of living areas.

Creating long-term support framework for development of  green transformation in participation regions, taking into consideration 3 BAUHAUS principles (sustainability, inclusiveness and aesthetics), with developed regional strategies and action plans
Improving policy impact and visibility of Euro-MED green transformation towards New European Bauhaus (NEB) Initiative.

Increasing visibility of Interreg Euro-MED programme in Brussels (European Commission) and by Euro-MED national decision makers.
en.actual_achievements:
The following tasks were implemented in PR1:

WP1: TESTING ADVANCED BAUHAUS PARTICIPATORY METHODS FOR THE GREEN TRANSFORMATION OF LIVING AREAS

The specific objective of WP1 is to test and develop sustainable, inclusive and aesthetic green solutions in partner-testing regions' living areas. Partnership work was focused on Activity 1, creating the internal deliverable "D1.1.0 Tailored Roadmap of participatory methods for enhancing development and testing NEB solutions". The Roadmap will be a tool to support our partner-testing regions and cities (PP2 RCM, PP3 SVEM, PP6 Regione Toscana, PP8 Sarajevo, PP9 Burgas) with digital and offline approaches, explaining how to engage local stakeholders and citizens in co-creating and co-developing green solutions. An essential aspect of our project is setting up the community dialogue and co-creation process with consideration of three NEB values: sustainable (in harmony with nature), together (connecting disciplines), and beautiful (inspired by local art and culture). To learn how to plan and implement participatory methods and how to use NEB values, task leader PP4 Platoniq, with the support of expert partners LP, PP5, PP7 and  PP10, prepared eight online training courses for all partners (on the 26th of March, 16th and 23rd of April, 9th and 21st of May, 4th, 11th and 18th of June 2024). The final two courses will be implemented in person as a workshop at the 2nd Project Group meeting in Burgas, Bulgaria (2nd and 3rd July 2024). The materials prepared for online courses present the content base for "D1.1.0 Roadmap"  which was finalised at the end of PR1.

WP2: ENSURING SUSTAINABILITY OF GREEN TRANSFORMATION

The specific objective of WP2 is to create a long-term support framework for the development of green transformation in five partner-testing regions (PP2 RCM, PP3 SVEM, PP6 Regione Toscana, PP8 Sarajevo, PP9 Burgas)  by setting up operational local nodes (networks) consisting of quintuple helix stakeholders. Nodes will include representatives of regional policy, academia, business, civil society and culture & creative sector representing the 5th "aesthetic" helix. The stakeholders of five local nodes will co-create regional strategies and action plans focused on green transformation, encompassing NEB principles and values.

In PR1,  project partners (PP2 RCM, PP3 SVEM, PP6 Regione Toscana, PP8 Sarajevo, PP9 Burgas), who are responsible for setting up the local nodes, as part of "Activity 2.1 Creating and operation of local nodes to evolve a Green Planetary Mindset" started to identify their key regional stakeholders. PP3 SVEM prepared a joint template report in which each partner will present the team of stakeholders and their competencies and roles.
As part of  "Activity 2.4 Contribution to the Results Amplification Strategy", Lead partner E-zavod, PP5 Crowdpolicy and PP6 Tuscany region participated in the Green Living Areas Thematic Community Kick-off event on the 10th and 11th of April 2024 in Turin, Italy,  presenting the project and identifying synergies with other community projects.

WP3: BAUHAUS4MED GOES BEYOND BORDERS

The specific objective of WP3 is improving the policy impact and visibility of Euro-MED green transformation towards the New European Bauhaus initiative and the visibility of the Interreg Euro-MED programme in the EU. Our expert partner, PP10 IFLA (European Region of the International Federation of Landscape Architects) started to implement tasks of "Activity 3.5 High-level BAUHAUS4MED visibility and dialogue with EU decision makers". Firstly, PP10 IFLA participated  and presented our project at the "New European Bauhaus Festival" on 9-13 April 2024 in Brussels, Belgium. Secondly, IFLA Europe Med Working Group and our project partners PP10 IFLA and PP2 RCM organised a conference and meeting, "Vulnerable landscapes - Towards green living landscapes", on 24-25 May 2024 in Athens, Greece.

MANAGEMENT

Project group meetings in person: the lead partner organised a kick-off project meeting in Bled, Slovenia, on 23.01.2024. The Steering Committee was set up at the kick-off meeting, and Steering Committee rules were presented. The second project group meeting is planned in Burgas, Bulgaria, on the 2nd and 3rd of July 2024.
Project task online meetings organised by lead partner and task leaders: 27th of February, 4th of March, 4th of April 2024 and 28th of May 2024.

COMMUNICATION

Communication coordinator PP5 Crowdpolicy developed and presented the project Communication Plan. The plan outlines future communication strategies, guidelines for publishing relevant material and information on social media, and the project branding manual. PP5 prepared the content for the project website and set up the social media channels (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and X).

In the first year of project implementation, the first steps were focused on preparation, how each city or region can adapt the values of the New European Bauhaus to local needs and address green urban challenges with citizens and stakeholders. To answer this, teams in Italy (Tuscany and Marche region), Bulgaria (Burgas), Bosnia and Herzegovina (Sarajevo), and Greece (Central Macedonia region) worked closely with local stakeholders to shape a common approach.
To support the dialogue and engagement at local levels, we developed a “CROWDVOCACY”, an online platform that allows citizens to share ideas, give feedback, and participate in designing greener and more liveable neighbourhoods. With this platform, each region has begun identifying the environmental challenges that matter most to local people, from heat islands and lack of shade to abandoned parks and underused public areas.
These findings are now feeding into a public call for citizen-designed solutions. Later this year, each region will run a competition inviting people to submit creative ideas for improving their green spaces—ideas that are sustainable, inclusive, and beautiful. The best ones will be tested and implemented on-site.
To ensure long-term change in approaching climate and green challenges, each region has also formed a local green transformation group called “LOCAL NODES”. These are diverse networks comprising representatives from government, education, business, civil society, and the creative sectors—all committed to keeping the momentum going.
Local nodes are playing an active role in our project. For example, representatives recently joined a Europe-wide session to share what they have learned and contribute to shaping future actions for greener living areas. 
Although focused on local communities, this project also aims to spark change at the European level. One of the project partners, the European Federation of Landscape Architects, has already begun presenting the initiative at major events across Europe, bringing the voices of our five regions and cities into conversations with national and European decision-makers.
With people at the centre and nature as a guiding principle, these regions and cities are transforming public spaces and shaping a new way of thinking about how we live together, care for our environment, and create a shared sense of beauty and belonging.


WP1: TESTING ADVANCED BAUHAUS PARTICIPATORY METHODS FOR THE GREEN TRANSFORMATION OF LIVING AREAS
The specific objective of WP1 is to test and develop sustainable, inclusive, and aesthetically pleasing green solutions in the living areas of partner-testing regions. Partnership work focused on finalising Activity 1.2, Identifying Green Transformation Community Challenges. A coordinator of the task, PP6 Regione Toscana, prepared an internal deliverable, D1.2.1, titled "Internal Deliverable Report on Green Transformation Challenges." The deliverable includes the presentation of identified turbulences, NEB categories related to challenges, a summary of activities realised and ongoing, NEB principles targeted, a presentation of local stakeholders, participatory tools used, and a time framework for the pilot site of the testing partners (PP2, PP3, PP6, PP8, PP9).
Additionally, Activity 1.3, the Citizens-driven BAUHAUS solutions competition, was implemented. All partner regions/cities, except PP2 RCM, published an open call on the project’s digital Crowdvocacy tool and invited citizens to provide solutions for the identified challenges. To ensure a joint project approach related to local open calls, task coordinators PP4 Platoniq, PP5 Crowdpolicy and PP7 AF ZG prepared an Open Call Handbook. This guide supports institutional partners in organising local Open Calls to gather citizen-driven, innovative solutions that address green transformation challenges in alignment with the New European Bauhaus (NEB) values: Sustainability, Aesthetics, and Inclusion. An internal deliverable, D1.3.1 Factsheet list of selected pilot projects, will be prepared in September 2025, once the PP2 RCM open call is finalised. The winning solutions of the local open calls will be implemented in Activity A1.4 Pilot implementation.

WP2: ENSURING SUSTAINABILITY OF GREEN TRANSFORMATION
The specific objective of WP2 is to create a long-term support framework for the development of green transformation in five partner-testing regions (PP2 RCM, PP3 SVEM, PP6 Regione Toscana, PP8 Sarajevo, PP9 Burgas).
In PR3, project partners (PP2 RCM, PP3 SVEM, PP6 Regione Toscana, PP8 Sarajevo, and PP9 Burgas) and the stakeholders of five local nodes, under the coordination of PP3 SVEM, started the process of co-creating regional strategies and action plans focused on green transformation, encompassing NEB principles and values. The first task of Activity 2.2 was to investigate existing policies and plans in regions and cities that support green transformation.
We also started with Activity A2.3 Local Bauhaus Festivals. The task leader, PP8 Sarajevo, organised an international Local Bauhaus Festival, titled “Green Transformation of Cities through New European Bauhaus,” on April 24, 2025. All PP members and stakeholders participated. Project partner PP3 SVEM organised a local Bauhaus festival on June 20, 2025. PP6 Regione Toscana organised a local Bauhaus Festival on June 14, 2025.
As part of "Activity 2.4 Contribution to the Results Amplification Strategy", the communication coordinator, PP5 Crowdpolicy, presented our project and participated in the networking event, the 2nd Gathering of the Green Living Areas Thematic Community, held in Barcelona on May 28-29. 2025. Lead partner E-zavod participated in the Green Living Areas territorial event in Ljubljana, Slovenia, on April 16, 2025.

WP3: BAUHAUS4MED goes beyond borders
The specific objective of WP3 is to improve the policy impact and visibility of the Euro-MED green transformation in relation to the New European Bauhaus initiative and enhance the visibility of the Interreg Euro-MED programme within the EU. 
Our expert partner, PP10 IFLA, organised the event “Med_net Working Group”, organised by IFLA in Rome, Italy, from 8th to 10th of May 2025. On the event-specific project workshop was organised, “Bauhaus4Med Med-net Workshop I and II - Green transition and Nature-Based Solutions implementation” on May 8 2025.

MANAGEMENT
Project group meetings in person: PP8 Sarajevo, together with the lead partner, organised a 4th project group meeting in Sarajevo on April 23, 2025.
Project task online meetings organised by the lead partner and task leaders: January 20, 4th and 31st of March 2025.

COMMUNICATION
The Communication Coordinator, PP5 Crowdpolicy, is leading the communication activities, including the preparation of articles, videos, and content for thematic areas. The website and social media are regularly updated.

fr.name: Tester de nouvelles solutions européennes du Bauhaus pour une transformation profondément verte des villes et des régions de la zone Euro-MED
fr.description:
En général l'écologie et la transformation verte sont considérées comme une obligation. Pour changer cette perception, l'initiative New European Bauhaus (NEB) a introduit des aspects esthétiques et la participation des citoyens pour soutenir la transformation verte urbaine.

Euro-MED BAUHAUS testera des solutions innovantes du NEB en intégrant la durabilité, l'inclusion et les aspects esthétiques dans la prise de décision pour améliorer les espaces verts. Les résultats du projet Interreg MED BLUE CROWDFUNDING seront mis à niveau dans un outil "CROWDVOCACY" avec l’aide du financement participatif, du crowdsourcing et de la démocratie participative, et d’une conception d'architecture verte esthétique apportant des solutions sur mesure pour améliorer le niveau de vie.

Chacune des 5 régions/villes à essai identifiera les défis communautaires verts participatifs, utilisera le crowdsourcing pour cocréer des solutions et lancera des campagnes de financement participatif civil pour les cofinancer, utilisera des budgets participatifs pour mettre en œuvre des solutions pilotes qui seront axées sur la construction circulaire, les espaces urbains verts régénérés, les produits verts et le style de vie vert. Une boîte à outils transférable comprendra un catalogue de meilleurs exemples, des leçons apprises et l'outil numérique CROWDVOCACY. Pour intégrer les principes du NEB et assurer la durabilité à long terme, les régions/villes élaboreront des plans d'action de transformation verte, en utilisant des principes verts, participatifs et esthétiques.

Le projet améliorera l'impact politique, la transférabilité et la durabilité des résultats et des livrables en organisant des événements au niveau régional et européen grâce à la position unique de PP10 IFLA Europe - membre du NEB COLLECTIVE :des conférences annuelles du NEB à Bruxelles, des régions rejoignant le NEB, un événement Transnational Green Transformation Challenge et la préparation des candidatures au NEB Rising Stars 2025.

en.name: Enhancing MED sustainable cultural tourism through the creation of eco-itineraries inside European Cultural Routes
en.description:
Cultural Tourism is a key asset in MED economies, however it has a strong, mutually detrimental relationship with climate change when tourism industries do not effectively incorporate sustainable practices. The MED area therefore needs to strategically re-shape its cultural tourism offer by matching its focus on local cultural heritage with greater sustainability. Cultural Routes of the Council of Europe (CRs) represent a key instrument to sustain and promote cultural tourism all over Europe, from mainstream destinations to small-scale local contexts. 
 
MEDRoutes overall objective is therefore to establish and disseminate a local-based, low-impact model of sustainable tourism across CRs in the MED area, in particular, to establish a specific model of Mediterranean "slow tourism" rooted in the common heritage of 4 CRs. 

To tackle the project general objective, MED-Routes’ approach is rooted in a TRANSFER rationale:
 -transfer mechanisms are put into place to cross-reference the most relevant, field-tested outputs from INCIRCLE & EMbleMatic projects for the design of eco-itineraries; 
-transfer mechanisms are further encouraged in application to MED-Routes experience through capacity building and institutional networking, especially in WP4. By exploiting Cultural Routes’ plural and transnational dimension, MED-Routes will encourage the diffusion of its eco-itinerary model in the context of other CRs and by the initiative of similar institutional actors transnationally.

The combination of all these elements defines transfer & capitalization as the core assets in MED-Routes’ approach in an innovative perspective for sharing the main outputs:
-Transnational Strategy for sustainable tourism & eco-itineraries in the MED area 
-Green Label of Mediterranean CRs for the certification of eco-itineraries
-MED-Routes Action Plans outlining key actions to apply the Transn. Strategy into CRs’ agenda.
en.expected_achievements:
Raise awareness among operators and stakeholders in tourism & in cultural services, as well as among CRs’ members, about the potential of sustainable tourism in targeted areas
Enrich the touristic offer of MED Cultural Routes through small-scale eco-itineraries oriented to circularity & sustainability

Increase the capacities of actors & operators inside the eco-itineraries to deliver their services and contribute to the popularity of sustainable tourism
Promote eco-itineraries among policy-makers & stakeholders by facilitating the adoption of the project’s vision in territorial agendas for sustainable growth and tourism
en.actual_achievements:
In PR1, MED-Routes successfully initiated activities to build awareness among tourism stakeholders and cultural services providers about the potential of sustainable tourism and increase understanding of how circular economy principles can be effectively applied to the cultural tourism sector in the MED region:

-State-of-the-Art Research (A1.1): This task involved a comprehensive mapping of existing eco-friendly and sustainable tourism activities across the targeted regions. Each partner contributed an integrated report detailing local policies, best practices, and key stakeholders. The result was the production of  Integrated Maps for the development of the local circular tourism sector (D.1.1.1), outlining the current state of circular tourism practices across the participating regions and opportunities for further development. The task was coordinated by BATTI, which was also in charge of finalizing and delivering the Integrated Maps.

-Preliminary analysis for the transferability of INCIRCLE and EMBLEMATIC Outputs (A1.2): One of MED-Routes' key strategies is to transfer successful methodologies from previous EU-funded projects INCIRCLE and EMBLEMATIC. In PR1, under LIBERTAS guidance, PPs conducted an in-depth analysis of these projects’ outputs, focusing on their applicability to MED-Routes’ goals. The result was a comprehensive  Catalogue of outputs to be transferred and capitalized from INCIRCLE and EMBLEMATIC (D.1.2.1), detailing tools such as the INCIRCLE Replicating Package and EMBLEMATIC Guidelines adapted for the creation of eco-itineraries and circular tourism models.

-Cross-Route Analysis and Transnational Working Group (A1.3):  Under BATTI supervision, a Transnational Working Group (TWG) was established to address the specific needs of each participating region, focusing on merging circular economy practices with cultural heritage valorization. PPs collaborated closely, meeting also in Varna (BG), using the INCIRCLE replicating package to explore solutions in waste management, water management, sustainable mobility, and renewable energy sources. A significant milestone was the development of the  Transnational Working Group Toolkit (D.1.3.1), which will guide future activities and help embed circular economy practices into the cultural tourism offerings of the four CRs.

-Carbon Footprint Calculation & Monitoring (A1.5 - in progress): BATTI designed a dedicated calculation tool to assess the carbon footprint of project activities, which was filled by PPs for PR1, providing relevant data for continuous evaluation of the project’s environmental impact.

-Synergies with EURO-MED Projects (A4.4 - in progress): To enhance cross-project cooperation, MED-Routes established a monitoring committee composed of 1 representative per PP to identify relevant EURO-MED projects for fostering synergies, dialogue and knowledge exchange.

As for the management & coordination tasks, in PR1 URF (LP) effectively coordinated the technical and financial oversight of the project. Internal communication was actively maintained, ensuring ongoing dialogue among partners and the JS. To facilitate collaboration, a shared Google Drive folder was established, providing PPs with access to project documentation and progress updates.

The Project Management Team (PMT) was established and led by URF, ensuring continuous technical and financial monitoring. Additionally, the Management Handbook was delivered, laying the foundation for smooth operations in future periods.

The consortium met in person at the KoM in Faenza (IT) on 7/2/24, during which the Steering Committee was established. A second TPM was held in Varna (BG) on 30/5/24, allowing partners to further discuss project progress and achievements.

Through this collaborative and well-structured management system, URF ensured the smooth technical and financial progression of the project according to schedule.
As for the communication activities, in PR1 INSULEUR (responsible PP) appointed a Project Communication Manager (PCM) to oversee all communication tasks, finalized the Project Communication Strategy and set up the Project Website, provided by the Programme, periodically updated with project news, events, and outputs in cooperation with PPs.
All PPs created dedicated sections on their institutional websites to display key project info, logo, and EU co-financing details, while MED-Routes posters were produced and displayed at partners’ premises to enhance project visibility and awareness. In addition, INSULEUR developed a Periodical Communication Monitoring Tool to track communication activities across all partners.
Partners contributed to the Result Amplification Strategy by participating in programme events: in particular, on the 10th and 11th of April 2024 the LP, together with INSULEUR’s PCM, participated in the KoM of the Interreg Euro-MED Sustainable Tourism Community in Rome, presenting MED-Routes and establishing contacts with other relevant EURO-MED projects.
In its 1st year, MED-Routes made significant progress in integrating sustainability & circular economy principles into cultural tourism across the Mediterranean. Through research, strategic stakeholder engagement, and transnational collaboration, the project successfully carried out key activities to enhance sustainability in CRs:
- State-of-the-Art Research (A1.1): PPs conducted a comprehensive assessment of sustainable tourism practices, mapping local policies, best practices & stakeholders, resulting in the Integrated Maps for the Development of the Local Circular Tourism Sector (D.1.1.1), providing insights into circular tourism opportunities.
- Transferability of INCIRCLE & EMBLEMATIC Outputs (A1.2): coordinate by LIU, PPs analyzed outputs from INCIRCLE & EMBLEMATIC to assess their relevance for MED-Routes. This led to the Catalogue of Outputs to be Transferred and Capitalized (D.1.2.1), identifying key methodologies for eco-itinerary development.
- Cross-Route Analysis and Transnational Working Group (A1.3): A Transnational Working Group (TWG) was formed under BATTI’s supervision, facilitating collaboration on waste management, sustainable mobility, and renewable energy solutions. The group developed the Transnational Working Group Toolkit (D.1.3.1) to support circular economy principles integration into CRs.
- Roadmap for CRs (A1.4): PPs collaborated with the four participating CRs to design tailored sustainability strategies, culminating in the Joint Roadmap for Cultural Routes (D.1.4.1). This document provides a structured framework for embedding circular practices in cultural tourism.
- Stakeholder Engagement and Participatory Labs (A2.1): Local participatory labs brought together tourism service providers, businesses, and community representatives to discuss sustainable tourism approaches. Insights were compiled in the Report of Consultation Groups (D.2.1.1), offering key recommendations for CRs.
- Development of Eco-Itineraries (A2.2): A Transnational Workshop in Pafos allowed PPs to co-design 2 eco-itineraries per CR, applying circular principles and methodologies from previous EU projects. The outcomes were documented in the Report of the Joint Transnational Workshop (D.2.2.1) and the EMBLEMATIC MED-Routes Transferability Guidelines (D.2.2.2).
- Green Label for CRs (A2.3 - ongoing): Work began on developing a Green Label for eco-itineraries, which will certify their sustainability. This label aims to increase visibility, encourage responsible tourism, and motivate stakeholders to adopt eco-friendly practices. 
- Synergies with EURO-MED Projects (A4.4 - ongoing): A monitoring committee was established to foster collaboration with relevant EURO-MED initiatives. MED-Routes representatives actively participated in multi-project seminars and clustering events, enhancing knowledge exchange and cross-project synergies.
- Carbon Footprint Calculation and Monitoring (A1.5 - Ongoing): Documents and tools for assessing activities’ carbon footprint have been established. PPs will consistently update them until the project's completion.
Concerning Management & Coordination, during PR1 & PR2 PPs were guided by the LP through the financial & technical reporting process, ensuring the timely submission of reports and certification of expenditures.
The consortium met in person at the KoM in Faenza (IT) on 7/2/24, where the StC was established. A 2nd TPM was held in Varna (BG) on 30/5/24, allowing PPs to further discuss project progress and achievements, while on 5/11/2024 the 3rd TPM was held in Pafos (CY), to discuss the state of the project, activities’ implementation, and technical/financial reporting, ensuring alignment with project objectives.
As for the communication activities, INSULEUR developed a comprehensive communication strategy focusing on promoting sustainable cultural tourism across the Mediterranean, through multimedia, stakeholder engagement, and transnational collaboration to raise awareness and encourage policy change. In addition, INSULEUR provided a Periodical Communication Monitoring Tool to collect information on partner-led communication activities, ensuring monitoring & overall coherence of the project’s dissemination efforts.
To enhance project visibility, MED-Routes posters were designed for display at partners’ premises. Additionally, a social media presence was established, with a Facebook account created and managed according to a structured social media calendar and guidelines produced by INSULEUR to ensure consistent outreach.
The project website was delivered and approved at the end of PR1. Throughout the second project period, the website was regularly updated with news, ensuring continuous engagement with stakeholders and the public. 
Throughout PR1 & PR2, MED-Routes demonstrated the added value of transnational cooperation in advancing circular economy principles within cultural tourism, paving the way for more sustainable & resilient tourism models across the Mediterranean.
In its third semester of activities, MED-Routes made steady progress in embedding sustainability and circular economy principles into cultural tourism throughout the Mediterranean region.

On May 19th and 20th, 2025 the Transnational Project Meeting (TPM) was held in Castellòn de la Plana, Spain. All activities to be conducted during this reporting period and the next one were discussed. 

Regarding WP1, throughout the semester, PPs continued to fill in the carbon footprint calculation and monitoring tool timely and carefully, accordingly to A1.5.

Regarding WP2, the consortium completed all activities successfully:
    - Concerning A2.2, PPs developed two fundamental documents to transfer the knowledge of the two Euro-MED projects that MED-Routes is capitalising, EMBLEMATIC and INCIRCLE: D2.2.2 EMBLEMATIC MED-Routes transferability guidelines for the creation of eco-itineraries, and D2.2.3 MED-Routes Package for sustainable practices and eco-itineraries in Med Cultural Routes. Building on this knowledge, and on previous activities conducted in the previous semesters, PPs were able to define all eight small-scale eco-itineraries foreseen by the project.
    - Concerning A2.3, the Green Label was officially finalized and presented to other Cultural Routes of the Council of Europe during the 12th Training Academy on Cultural Routes, held on June 13, 2025, in Torres Vedras, Portugal
    - Concerning A2.4, each Cultural Route developed a promotional strategy for its eco-itineraries, with the goal of increasing the visibility of the eco-itinerary and consolidating its identity.
    - Concerning A2.5, the Transnational Strategy for sustainable tourism and eco-itineraries was delivered. The Strategy was developed by integrating 4 project deliverables (D2.2.2, D2.2.3, D2.3.1, D2.4.1), to provide a comprehensive framework for the development, promotion, and potential replication of the MED-Routes eco-itineraries. To ensure broader accessibility, the Strategy was translated into the languages of all partners developing eco-itineraries. Finally, a dedicated Letter of Intent was co-developed and signed by all CRs involved in the project. This document reaffirms their shared commitment to adopting the Strategy as a reference framework within their policies. 

Regarding WP3, during this reporting period, partners developed training materials for the dissemination of eco-itineraries (A3.1). 

Regarding WP4, during this reporting period, partners worked on some activities:
    - Concerning Activity 4.1, PPs and APs started to plan how to build the MED-Routes Action Plans in the next reporting period;
    - Concerning Activity 4.2, PPs started to plan the Forums they will hold next semester;
    - Concerning Activity 4.3, PPs presented the MED-Routes approach and products during the Training Academy of the Cultural Routes taking place in Torres Vedras (PT) from June, 11th to June, 13th 2025. 
    - Concerning A4.4, PPs attended online meetings with the Euro-MED community and planned activities for cross fertilization with other projects.

Lastly, PPs put efforts in communicating the project, in accordance with the Communication Strategy.
During the last semester, MED-Routes clearly showcased the benefits of transnational collaboration in promoting circular economy approaches within the cultural tourism sector, setting the foundations for more sustainable and resilient tourism models throughout the Mediterranean.

fr.name: Enhancing MED sustainable cultural tourism through the creation of eco-itineraries inside European Cultural Routes
fr.description:
Le tourisme culturel est un atout essentiel des économies méditerranéennes, qui entretien une relation en même temps forte mais mutuellement préjudiciable avec le changement climatique lorsque les industries du tourisme n'appliquent pas des pratiques durables. L'espace MED doit donc remodeler sa strategie d’offre touristique culturelle en mettant l’accent sur la durabilité du patrimoine culturel. Les Itinéraires Culturels du Conseil de l'Europe (IC) représentent un instrument clé pour soutenir et élancer le tourisme culturel en Europe, toutes destinations incluses, grandes et petites.

L'objectif global de MED-Routes est donc d'établir et de diffuser un modèle de tourisme durable à faible impact et à base locale à travers les IC de l'espace MED et d'établir un modèle spécifique de 'slow tourism' méditerranéen, enraciné dans le patrimoine commun des 4 IC.

Pour atteindre son objectif, l'approche du Projet est ancrée à une logique de TRANSFERT:
- des mécanismes de transfert sont établis pour croiser les résultats les plus pertinents des projets INCIRCLE et EMbleMatic avec la conception des éco-itinéraires; 
- des mécanismes de transfert sont encouragés par le renforcement des capacités et la mise en réseau institutionnelle, spécialement en WP4. En exploitant la dimension pluraliste et transnationale des IC, MED-Routes encouragera la diffusion du modèle d'éco-itinéraires dans le contexte d'autres IC et par les initiatives d'acteurs institutionnels similaires et transnationaux.

Ces éléments associés font du transfert et de la capitalisation les atouts principaux de l'approche de MED-Routes pour une perspective innovante de partage des résultats:

-une stratégie transnationale pour le tourisme durable et les éco-itinéraires dans l'espace MED

-un label vert des IC méditerranéens pour la certification des éco-itinéraires

-des plans d'action pour les MED-Routes décrivant les actions clés pour appliquer la Stratégie Transnational dans l'agenda des IC.
fr.expected_achievements:
Raise awareness among operators and stakeholders in tourism & in cultural services, as well as among CRs’ members, about the potential of sustainable tourism in targeted areas
Enrich the touristic offer of MED Cultural Routes through small-scale eco-itineraries oriented to circularity & sustainability

Increase the capacities of actors & operators inside the eco-itineraries to deliver their services and contribute to the popularity of sustainable tourism
Promote eco-itineraries among policy-makers & stakeholders by facilitating the adoption of the project’s vision in territorial agendas for sustainable growth and tourism

fr.name: Suivi des eaux souterraines et développement d'un outil d'aide à la décision pour optimiser la prise de décision dans les environnements agricoles sensibles et pauvres en eau en contexte méditerranéen
fr.description:
Une clepsydre est une horloge à eau, où l'eau mesure le temps et, dans ce projet c'est l'eau qui sera "mesurée" dans le temps. En effet, il existe un besoin en amélioration des réseaux de surveillance des aquifères conventionnels, contrôlant principalement la zone saturée de l'aquifère et ayant une valeur limitée pour évaluer l'impact des mesures de prévention de la pollution. Ensuite, par le manque de convivialité des modèles numériques d'écoulement utilisés comme outils de prévision. Il est donc tout aussi crucial de disposer d'un outil intelligent pour interpréter les données hydrogéologiques que de sensibiliser la société civile à l'importance des eaux souterraines.

Clepsydra vise à développer et tester un système intelligent de surveillance et d'interprétation des données hydrogéologiques sur le comportement des aquifères, y compris développer un système d'aide à la décision - DSS, avec une attention particulière à la qualité des eaux souterraines en agriculture. En améliorant les réseaux de surveillance actuels et en développant un DSS, les professionnels de l'eau et de l'agriculture pourront prendre des décisions respectueuses de l'environnement sur la base de données scientifiques fiables. 

Dans le projet, la coopération et l’implication des parties prenantes de la quadruple hélice sont un principe directeur pour générer des résultats à fort impact. Le projet implique 9 partenaires de 6 pays Med (PT, ES, FR, IT, MT & EL), représentant la sphère publique, académique, agricole, des utilisateurs d’eaux souterraines et un cluster de l'eau. Dans les sites pilotes, le projet Clepsydra exploitera le potentiel d'innovation du DSS et l'alignera sur les pratiques améliorées de surveillance des eaux souterraines. Les parties prenantes de la 4helix seront impliquées dans le projet et des partenariats public-privé seront générés avec l'implication des utilisateurs finaux et du secteur privé dans l'amélioration des réseaux de surveillance.
fr.expected_achievements:
To benchmark best practices in the Mediterranean piloting area and conclusions to reduce the gap in the project study cases. 

To standardise and improve the groundwater monitoring systems in the pilot sites and to develop a Decision Support System (DSS) to guide groundwater and agricultural professionals in their managing tasks.


To test in real conditions in the 4 pilot sites (3 partners’ countries) the improved groundwater monitoring networks and the use of the DSS.


To increase institutional capacity of organisation working with groundwater management and the agriculture sector and to enhance the whole understanding of general society about the aquifers in the Mediterranean area.

en.name: Clepsydra - Groundwater monitoring and Decision Support System development to optimise decision-making in sensitive and water-scarce agricultural environments in the Mediterranean context
en.description:
A clepsydra is a water clock, where water measures the time and, in this project it is the water which will be "measured" in time. In fact, there is a need to improve conventional aquifers monitoring networks which mainly control aquifer saturated zone, having a limited value for the assessment of the impact of pollution prevention measures taken. Additionally, a smart tool to interpret hydrogeological data is needed because current numerical flow models to predict groundwater pollution and level is not friendly for all users. Also, raising awareness of civil society about the importance of aquifers is needed.

Clepsydra aims to develop and test a smart system for monitoring and interpreting hydrogeological data on the behaviour of aquifers (including the development of a Decision Support System - DSS), with special attention to groundwater quality in agriculture to be able to make decisions. By improving current monitoring networks and developing a DSS, water and agriculture professionals will be able to make environmentally friendly decisions based on reliable scientific data.

In the project, the increased cooperation and capacities of the 4helix stakeholders is a guiding principle to generate project results with greater impact. The project involves 9 partners from 6 Med countries (PT, ES, FR, IT, MT & EL), made up of public authorities, academia, agriculture, groundwater users and a water cluster. 

In the pilot sites, Clepsydra Project will harness the innovation potential of the DSS and align it with upgraded groundwater monitoring practices. 4helix stakeholders will be involved in the project and public-private partnerships will be generated with the incorporation of final users and private sector in the improvement of monitoring networks.
en.expected_achievements:
To benchmark best practices in the Mediterranean piloting area and conclusions to reduce the gap in the project study cases. 

To standardise and improve the groundwater monitoring systems in the pilot sites and to develop a Decision Support System (DSS) to guide groundwater and agricultural professionals in their managing tasks.


To test in real conditions in the 4 pilot sites (3 partners’ countries) the improved groundwater monitoring networks and the use of the DSS.


To increase institutional capacity of organisation working with groundwater management and the agriculture sector and to enhance the whole understanding of general society about the aquifers in the Mediterranean area.
en.actual_achievements:
During the first reporting period, our team made significant progress across several key activities, contributing to the overall objectives of Clepsydra Project. We focused on establishing strong coordination and communication channels among all partners to ensure smooth collaboration. This included participating in regular virtual meetings, setting up communication and document sharing protocols and ensuring all partners were aligned with the project’s objectives and timelines.

The implementation of the work programme was focused on WP1 on assessing of monitoring capacities in pilot areas and identification of key gaps. In fact, partners where pilot will be implemented, have started to select the members of their local living labs. We also engaged with stakeholders through virtual communications and meetings, gathering valuable feedback that helped shape the project’s approach and outputs. On the other hand, partners agreed on the approach and template to be filled in about the comparative analysis of the control and monitoring of existing networks in pilot sites whose results will be the base for the design of the following project activities. In parallel, partners also made the first steps to calculate the Water and carbon footprint of the control. In addition to that, Clepsydra has started to design the Groundwater Open Days which will be finally implemented in the following semester. Overall, our progress during this period has laid a solid foundation for the continued success of Clepsydra Project.

During the first year, the Clepsydra project has made steady and substantial progress, moving from initial coordination activities to the first technical and preparatory steps toward achieving its core objectives. The project aims to develop an innovative, AI-based Decision Support System (DSS) and improve groundwater monitoring networks to enhance decision-making in water-scarce agricultural environments across the Mediterranean. The progress achieved so far reflects the strength of transnational cooperation, with each partner contributing expertise, data, and insights to ensure shared goals are met.

The first phase of the project focused on building a solid foundation for collaboration. The partnership established strong internal communication structures, including monthly virtual coordination meetings, efficient document-sharing platforms, and clear timelines to keep all partners aligned. 

In Work Package 1 (WP1), the focus was on assessing the monitoring capacities in pilot areas and identifying key gaps. With the coordination of Aqua Valley, partners leading pilot sites set up their Local Living Labs (LLLs), bringing together stakeholders from public authorities, the private sector, research institutions, and civil society. The first meetings of these LLLs have been crucial in gathering feedback and ensuring that project actions respond to real local needs. Partners also collaborated to prepare a comparative analysis of existing monitoring networks in each pilot area led by IGME-CSIC, a task that will feed directly into the design of future monitoring protocols and DSS functionality. Parallel to this, partners began calculating the water and carbon footprints associated with current monitoring practices, supporting the project’s commitment to sustainability. Another notable achievement in WP1 was the launch of the Groundwater Open Days, with most partners successfully organizing these public events to raise awareness of groundwater issues among local communities.
In Work Package 2 (WP2), key progress has been made in designing the Decision Support System (DSS), which is the project’s main technical output. All partners contributed by providing critical data from their pilot sites and defining the main caracheristics of the tool, enabling UNICAS to start developing the predictive models. UNICAS has already made significant headway by testing several AI-based models, focusing particularly on forecasting groundwater levels (GWL), identified as the most relevant parameter for the DSS. This collaborative approach highlights the added value of transnational cooperation, as data collected from diverse Mediterranean environments enriches the accuracy and robustness of the models.
In Work Package 3 (WP3), after conducting the detailed assessments of existing monitoring networks, partners identified key gaps and began planning improvements. Preparations are underway for acquiring and installing new monitoring instruments, tailored to the specific needs of each pilot site. Additionally, partners continued soil monitoring in agricultural areas, maintaining consistent data collection that will feed into the DSS. 

Work Package 4 (WP4) also saw early but important progress, led by IRD. The focus has been on laying the groundwork for capacity building and raising social awareness. Activities have included the selection of study sites, mapping stakeholder profiles, and starting the development of educational materials and video content aimed at explaining groundwater management challenges and solutions in an accessible way. IRD’s work ensures that the project’s technical results are not confined to specialists but are translated into messages that can reach broader audiences and help drive behavior change.

Communication activities have advanced significantly, with key tools developed. Aqua Valley has finalised the project’s communication strategy, created poster and roll-up templates, launched the project website and social media channels, and begun work on the first project video. This communication effort ensures that both project stakeholders and the general public are kept informed and engaged.

A highlight of this period was the project’s presence at an influential international event: IGME-CSIC and EWA’s participation in the Water Framework Directive:  46th CIS-Groundwater Working Group Meeting in Budapest (November 2024). Presenting Clepsydra at this high-level forum allowed the project to gain visibility among policymakers and experts, demonstrating the relevance of the project’s objectives and approach within the broader European water governance context.

During this reporting period, one key deviation from the original work plan concerned the delay in finalising Deliverable D2.1.1 – Groundwater Monitoring Protocol for the Pilot Sites. After starting the drafting process, it became evident that the development of this protocol is significantly more complex than initially anticipated. The protocol must be applicable across four pilot sites with very different hydrogeological, climatic, and operational characteristics. As a result, it is essential to carefully adapt technical content to ensure the recommendations are relevant, feasible, and scientifically sound in each local context.

Moreover, the protocol must go beyond general guidelines. It is expected to harmonise diverse technical methodologies, sampling procedures, and data quality criteria that meet both regulatory requirements and research standards. Given this level of complexity, the responsible team, led by IGME-CSIC, made the decision to prioritise the technical robustness and coherence of the document over speed of delivery. Work on the protocol continues actively, and a final version is expected in the coming months. The partnership appreciates the Joint Secretariat’s understanding, as this delay reflects the ambition to produce a high-quality and impactful result.

On the financial side, some delays in spending have also been observed compared to the original forecast. These deviations are mainly due to the longer-than-expected duration and complexity of public procurement procedures for equipment acquisition. As a result, certain planned expenses have been postponed. Nevertheless, all procurement processes are now underway or nearing completion. IGME-CSIC, as project lead partner, is closely monitoring the financial execution of each partner and their updated forecasts for the upcoming semesters. This will help ensure alignment with the overall budget and allow corrective measures to be taken early if needed to safeguard project performance.

fr.name: Captage et stockage du CO2 atmosphérique pour améliorer la qualité des sols
fr.description:
Deux grands défis actuels sont : comment réduire le CO2 dans l'atmosphère et comment préserver la qualité du sol. Trop de carbone dans l'air provoque l'effet de serre et trop peu de carbone dans le sol réduit sa qualité.
Le projet préparera les bases scientifiques pour réduire le CO2 dans l'air et le stocker dans le sol en utilisant des techniques de culture du carbone, un sujet innovant dans l'UE ; sa politique ayant émergé en 2021 il est donc urgent de définir rapidement des normes, des procédures de surveillance et des modèles socio-économiques pour l’avenir.
Le projet CARBON 4 SOIL QUALITY préparera une boîte à outils pour la culture du carbone comprenant : une liste de valeurs organiques du sol ; une méthodologie pour analyser le carbone organique et le suivi de la qualité du sol ; un guide des techniques de culture du carbone ; des modèles commerciaux d'agriculture du carbone ; des recommandations pour le crédit carbone agricole et la certification environnementale.

Un plan d'action sera préparé au niveau transnational pour planifier l’essai envisageant au moins 5 sites dans différents pays, dont au moins un pays IAP et un nouveau territoire Interreg Euro-MED (Bulgarie, Estrémadure). Une coopération transnationale est nécessaire pour étudier différentes zones climatiques méditerranéennes (subtropicales, océaniques, semi-arides, arides), différentes conditions d'érosion et différents types de sols. 
Le suivi du projet testera les techniques de culture du carbone pour augmenter la matière organique du sol ce qui améliorera les éléments nutritifs des plantes, augmentera le stockage de l'eau et fournira un habitat aux organismes pour améliorer la biodiversité du sol.

La culture du carbone est considérée comme l'une des actions climatiques du Green Deal européen et les politiques européennes concernées seront développées entre 2023 et 2030. Ce projet, s'il est approuvé, deviendra un exemple phare de la réduction du CO2 de l’Euro-MED et un « showcase » pour l'UE.

en.name: Capturing and Storing Atmospheric CO2 for Improvement of Soil Quality
en.description:
Two of the biggest challenges of human civilization are how to reduce CO2 in the atmosphere and how to preserve the soil quality. Too much carbon in the air causes greenhouse effect, and too little carbon in the soil reduces soil quality. 

Proposed project will prepare scientific foundations for reducing CO2 in the air and storing it into the soil, using SUSTAINABLE SOIL MANAGEMENT, called "CARBON FARMING". Since carbon farming is innovative topic in EU (policy emerged in 2021), there is an urgent need to quickly define standards, monitoring procedures and socio-economic models to test carbon farming in future. 

CARBON 4 SOIL QUALITY project will prepare "Toolbox for carbon farming" consisting of (i) Catalogue of soil organic reference values, (ii) Methodology for organic carbon analysis and soil quality monitoring (iii), Guidelines for carbon farming techniques, (iv) carbon farming business models, (v) Recommendations on agriculture carbon credit schemes and environmental certification systems.

Action plan will be prepared on the transnational level to properly plan testing phase. It will consider at least 5 testing sites in different countries, including at least one IPA country and one new Interreg Euro-MED territory. 

Transnational cooperation is needed for studying different Mediterranean climate zones (sub-tropical, oceanic, semi-arid, arid), different erosion conditions and different types of soil.

Project follow-up will test carbon farming techniques to support farmers with practical knowledge, how to increase soil organic matter. This will improve plant nutrients, increase soil water storage and provide good habitat for soil living organisms, which enhances soil biodiversity. 

Carbon farming is considered as one of the European Green Deal climate actions and related EU policies will be developed in the 2023-2030 period. If approved, this project could become SUSTIAINABLE SOIL MANAGEMENT lighthouse example of Euro-MED territory and showcase for the EU.
en.expected_achievements:
Work package aims at improving missing knowledge gaps about carbon farming and its effects on soil quality and climate change.

As a result of the study and research, the project will offer a toolbox for testing carbon farming in Euro-MED area. 
Work package will prepare solid base for future testing of carbon farming in the Euro-MED area.

This will be achieved with preparation of feasible action plan, training material, improved visibility and policy connections for future cooperation.
Work package will have 2 objectives:
1.) Monitoring carbon footprint and ensuring carbon-offsetting actions;
2.) Contribution to the Results Amplification Strategy and to the Programme activities.
en.actual_achievements:
Management Activities:
In the first reporting period, all project partners familiarized themselves with the project application form. A kick-off meeting was organized and attended by all partners, where the project framework, management, communication, and reporting activities were presented. The project timeline, deliverables, and roles and responsibilities of the partners were outlined. Several online meetings were held to discuss WP1 activities and management and communication tasks.

Communication Activities:
The communication strategy was prepared, and participation in communication manager training was completed. A presentation outlining communication responsibilities for partners was developed. Social media channels (LinkedIn and Facebook) were managed, and partners were coordinated for post publishing. A press release about the kick-off meeting was prepared, and the official project website was launched. All partners posted the project poster and mentioned the project on their official websites.

WP1 Activities:

Activity 1.1: Soil organic matter reference values for different soil types were defined through the incorporation of relevant documents and online meetings to achieve deliverable D1.1.1: Catalogue of Soil Organic Matter Reference Values.

Activity 1.2: Organic carbon analysis standards and monitoring procedures were adapted through the use of provided documents and online meetings to achive deliverable D1.2.1: Methodology for Organic Carbon Analysis and Soil Quality Monitoring.

Activity 1.4: Carbon farming socio-economic models were defined, and a plan for secondary research on the socio-economic impacts of carbon farming, including sustainability aspects, was prepared.

Implementation of WP3:

Activity 3.1: Participation in the Carbon Footprint Coordinators Group was completed, and the Carbon Footprint Monitoring and Assessment Tool was reviewed. A presentation with an action plan for all project partners was prepared.

Management
-Organised and implemented the kick-off meeting in Bled, Slovenia.
-Organised and implemented the project meeting in Bar, Montenegro.
-Initiated collaboration with other Euro-MED projects, fostering synergies and exploring opportunities for joint activities.

Communication
-Produced and published the official project video.
-Completed and regularly updated the project website with relevant news, documents, and event announcements.
-Established and maintained active social media channels (LinkedIn and Facebook), sharing project content and promoting visibility.
-Participated in events organised by the Natural Heritage Mission, enhancing capitalisation and knowledge exchange.
-Developed and implemented the project’s communication strategy, including key messages, target audiences, and dissemination tools.

Implementation of WP1
Activity 1.1 – Definition of Soil Organic Matter (SOM) Reference Values
We started with the development of the Catalogue of Soil Organic Matter Reference Values by reviewing proposed templates and methodologies. Input data were gathered, reviewed, and aligned with regional datasets to ensure consistency across the Euro-MED area. The deliverable was finalised based on shared contributions and joint coordination.

Activity 1.2 – Adaptation of Organic Carbon Analysis Standards
We began implementing this activity by reviewing the proposed methodology and ensuring its compatibility with regional soil monitoring systems. The draft document was reviewed and finalised in collaboration with expert inputs. A common approach for organic carbon analysis and quality monitoring was established.

Activity 1.4 – Definition of Carbon Farming Socio-Economic Models
We launched the activity by drafting the main concept and outlining the structure of the socio-economic models. A preliminary document was prepared, defining four model types (within and outside the agri-food chain, farm level, and public sector). Reporting templates were developed to collect structured input from all partners. Work continues on refining the models with a sustainability focus.

Implementation of WP2
Activity 2.1 – Strategic Framework for Soil Quality Improvement
We started the development of a strategic approach to improve soil quality in Mediterranean climates. A draft protocol and methodology were prepared, including a PEST analysis (Political, Economic, Social, Technological) to guide contributions from all regions involved. Initial discussions focused on aligning soil management practices with regional policy contexts.

Activity 2.2 – Development of Training Materials for Carbon Farming
We prepared training content aimed at agricultural advisors and practitioners. The materials cover key topics such as soil quality, the carbon cycle, carbon farming principles, and carbon credit schemes. An online course format was proposed, including presentations, brochures, videos, and interactive elements such as quizzes and a glossary.

Activity 2.5 – Policy Impact Assessment
We initiated dialogue with national stakeholders to assess the project’s relevance at both European and regional levels. Efforts focused on raising awareness of sustainable soil management practices and collecting feedback through meetings and consultations. The groundwork for long-term policy integration of carbon farming was established.

Implementation of WP3
Activity 3.1 – Monitoring and Offset of the Project Carbon Footprint
We started the implementation of this activity by preparing a detailed action plan and communication tools for project partners. Data for the first and second reporting periods were collected, verified, and entered into the official Carbon Footprint Monitoring Tool. We participated in the Carbon Footprint Coordinators Group meeting and provided supporting materials, including a presentation and shared Excel templates to ensure methodological coherence.
During the third reporting period, partners worked jointly on different project activities. A project meeting was held in Skopje, North Macedonia, between 21–23 January 2025.

The project website and social media channels were regularly updated with relevant and engaging content. In addition, partners actively participated in external events where the project topic was of interest, presenting project results and working on increasing its visibility and recognition.

Within WP1, we successfully completed Activity 1.3 – Research about carbon farming techniques most appropriate for the Euro-MED area and finalized Deliverable 1.3.1 – Guidelines for carbon farming techniques. Furthermore, we initiated the implementation of Activity 1.5 – Study of carbon credit schemes and environmental certification systems.

Within WP2, the following milestones were achieved:

Completion of Activity 2.2 – Preparation of transferable carbon farming training materials and submission of Deliverable 2.2.1 – Training material for carbon farming.

Completion of Activity 2.3 – Planning actions for testing carbon farming schemes in the Euro-MED area and submission of Deliverable 2.3.1 – Action plan for testing carbon farming.

Currently, preparations for the Final Conference are ongoing.

fr.name: Transformative co-creation innovation labs for the Blue Sustainable Economy
fr.description:
BLUE ECOSYSTEM expérimentera des méthodologies innovantes centrées sur le soutien des processus de création de territoires via la production de connaissances dans l'économie bleue durable, en s'appuyant sur les expériences existantes réalisées par précédents projets MED ( MISTRAL, BLUE BIO MED et B-BLUE). Le partenariat solide et synergique, dirigé par Region Emilia-Romagna, garantira des résultats efficaces et des tentatives concrètes vers une économie bleue plus durable et résiliente dans la région méditerranéenne. L'approche du cadre conceptuel partagé avec le projet Dialogue4Innovation garantit un impact plus large sur le dialogue institutionnel et la gouvernance des politiques d'innovation. Le projet concevra, testera et validera:


1.un laboratoire d'innovation de co-création transformative (TRAIN labs) dans chaque région pilote : Île Baléares, Émilie-Romagne, Sud Paca-Occitanie, Comté de Zadar, Alentejo, Attique, Albanie, en cohérence avec les plans d'action régionaux sur l'économie bleue.

2.un programme BLUE ECOMATCH: un processus transnational d'innovation ouverte pour relever les défis complexes de la région MED et pour identifier les opportunités transnationales afin de fournir plus rapidement des solutions. 

3.un livre sur la transformation de l'économie bleue durable: les principaux résultats et les meilleures pratiques pour l'apprentissage mutuel de l'innovation transformative.L'utilisation de la méthodologie TRAIN Labs, soutenue par des initiatives d'apprentissage mutuel telles que BLUE ECOMATCH, permettra un changement d'état d'esprit des parties prenantes de la quintuple hélice et des acteurs clés des économies bleues, en faveur d’une utilisation plus large de l'innovation, générant enfin l'adoption de modèles commerciaux plus durables dans le secteur privé, le développement de nouvelles compétences sur les marchés des secteurs bleus et contribuant à la suppression des barrières institutionnelles et culturelles qui entravent l'innovation avancée des économies bleues.
fr.expected_achievements:
To co–design territorial transformative co-creation innovation labs in each participating territory through a common shared methodology, to enhance the capacity of territories to work on transformative innovation projects 

Test and implement 8 territory-based transformative co-creation innovation labs aiming at developing an innovation portfolio of solutions to co-designed challenges.


WP3 aims at setting up a joint evaluation process not only of project efficacy but also of the value of outcomes & spillovers generated by TRAIN lab methodology for its empowerment at the policy level, as leverage to a wide adoption in Blue Economies

en.name: Transformative co-creation innovation labs for the Blue Sustainable Economy
en.description:
BLUE ECOSYSTEM will test innovative methodologies focused on supporting territory-making processes through knowledge production in the blue sustainable economy, building on existing experiences achieved thanks to previous MED projects ( MISTRAL, Blue BIo MED and B-BLUE). The strong and complementary partnership, led by Emilia-Romagna Region, will assure effective results and concrete attempts towards a more sustainable and resilient blue economy in the Mediterranean area. Shared conceptual framework approach with Dialogue4Innovation project guarantees a wider impact on institutional dialogue and innovation policy governance. BLUE ECOSYSTEM will design, test and validate:
- a Transformative co-creation innovation labs (TRAIN labs) in each pilot region: Balearic Island, Emilia-Romagna, Sud Paca-Occitanie, Zadar County, Alentejo, Attica, Albania, in coherence with regional action plans on blue economy.
- a BLUE ECOMATCH program: a MED transnational open innovation process to address MED complex challenges and match, connect and identify transnational opportunities for the solution acceleration;
- a Transformative Book for Blue sustainable economy, sharing the overall project approach, the main outcomes and best practices for the mutual learning about Transformative innovation.
A wide use of TRAIN Lab methodology, supported by initiatives of mutual learning such as the BLUE ECOMATCH program, will make possible a change in the mindset of 5 helix stakeholders and key-players of the Blue economies, towards a wider use of co-designed innovation and cutting edge technologies, lastly generating the adoption of more sustainable business models in the private sector, the development of new skills in the blue sectors markets and contributing to the removal institutional and cultural barriers that hamper advanced innovation in blue economies.
