Next steps foreseen will be the design and launch of calls, to start implementing accelleration processes and next destination to reach will be Albania with new initiatives that will be announced very soon!  
In the semester concerned, all PPs have actively participated in the meetings arranged online, to guarantee a smooth and well-integrated development and implementation of the actions foreseen. Similarly, several meetings have been arranged, with specific focus on Communication (24 January, 8 April, and 30 May) which is a relevant part of the project, supporting dissemination of technical activities, events' participation and networking. A PP meeting has taken place in person in Durres (8th-9th April 2025), hosted by PP8 and involving PPS' Associated partners, with the participation of JS. In terms of technical activities, specific efforts have been devoted in the development of the "calls for solutions" after an intense work that the partnership has put in place, identifying specific territorial challenges in the driving strategic blue sectors selected in the previous semester. A deep analysis has driven to shape the elaboration and publication of calls, supported by territorial meetings whereas key stakeholders of the quintuple helix have been involved and whose contributions have been collected. These activities have enriched numbers and typology of stakeholders involved (AT1.3) and have launched the basis for the acceleration process within the TRAIN LABS together with the selection of solutions that are foreseen in period 4, 5 and 6. The partnership has agreed to have a PP meeting in Lanarca (Cyprus) the 8th and 9th of July, to better monitor and exchange information about the outcomes of the publications of the calls. The activities implemented so far, have enabled to gain important elements to access step by step the outcomes, also in relation to S3 (AT3.1, AT3.2) carrying on also the networking via the "Blue Task Force" presented at Durres meeting, and involving Interreg Euro-MED Innovative Sustainable Economy Mission, the Horizon Europe BlueMissionMed project, with the support of the IMC (AT3.3, AT3.4). Blue Ecosystem has been presented in several events: 18th February 2025 "INFORMAL GROUP ON THE BLUE DIMENSION OF S3" that has driven to deliver a Policy paper for an impactful S3 (involving RER and CPRM), 3rd Community of Practice (COP) of the ISE Mission in Sarajevo (April 2025), and the Mediterranean Day of the United Nations Ocean Conference (UNOC) held in Nice (June 2025) involving PMM-TVT, CPRM and HCMR, AZA4ICE project during the Circle the Med Forum in Athens (June 4, 2025) and during the European Bioremediation Forum in Chania, Greece (June 15–19, 2025), held under the auspices of the EU Mission “Restore our Ocean and Waters” and DG RTD OCEAN, involving HCMR. Also, for the period concerned, data about the carbon footprint have been duly collected (by CPRM) and made available to all PPs.

en.name: REPair PERspective
en.description:
Why not? Re-thinking traditional business models, re-discovering the centrality of human value in the pursuit of innovation and competitiveness, re-viewing behaviors, all toward a circular economy model, is more urgent than ever.
Why not? To support SMEs in the repair economy, increase repair skills, and pursue the transition to more sustainable choices by overcoming the existing repair barriers. 
The REPper project aims to address these challenges and propose concrete solutions to meet them.
A global challenge such as sustainability, by reducing waste, increasing reuse, adopting regenerative practices and - heart of the project- implementing repair, can only be met by acting on a transnational scale.
How? Through 3 action areas strongly focused on cross-border cooperation: 

1. Setting-up a REPper HUBS NETWORK - a unique contact point, physical and/or digital - with the aim of designing and testing a transnational network of pilot services. 

2. Creating a REPper FACTORY for upskilling and reskilling, providing training at the highest EU level, to reduce imbalances between Mediterranean regions. 

3. Building a new repair culture through "gentle nudges" - NUDGING - that can trigger positive changes and new habits. 
These actions will inspire joint solutions such as business model canvas, blueprint, and Nudge; strategies and action plans (Roadmap, recommendations, and policy papers); and, finally, cooperative outputs with an eye to the future: an e-pledge and a letter of interest. 
To do this, REPper brings together highly qualified and diversified partners, capable of capitalizing on the past results, implementing them with new actions for public and private targets: SMEs, public authorities, sectoral agencies, business support organizations, schools and, last but not least, citizens. 
And if "Why not?" is a question that offers multiple solutions bringing us closer to a Greener Europe, REPper wants to take up the challenge by offering a different perspective.
en.expected_achievements:
To design and test a transnational network of pilot services to support new enterprises in the repair sector
To fertilize the ground for up-skill and re-skill professionals by 1. developing a transnational repair Factory, 2. preparing skills portfolios for repair trainers and business supporter, 3. training-the-trainers 
To mobilise decision makers and reinforce the trust and attitude of consumers in repairing trough nudging
en.actual_achievements:
In the initial phase of the project, MCBO took the lead in defining the operational and organizational structure. It established detailed guidelines for financial tracking, administrative setup, website adaptation, and coordinated communication activities. Additionally, MCBO integrated a shared folder system to optimize filing and task tracking. At the same time, it managed the contractual aspects by coordinating all partners to ensure timely updates of the necessary documentation on the JEMS platform. The partners (PP) followed MCBO's guidelines, adopting the established procedures and contributing to the smooth progression of the project from the outset.
During the first period of the project, several activities were carried out across different WPs, focusing on the design, testing, and implementation of the REPper Hubs and engagement with key stakeholders. The start of period 1 took place with the Kick off meeting in Lisbon on 5 and 6 March 2024.
In WP1, partners worked on developing the network of pilot services to support enterprises in the repair sector. Based on the feasibility and operational plans prepared by E-institute, partners assessed the characteristics and services of their regional hubs and shared knowledge about best practices. MCBO as LP, supported E-Zavod in creating a common methodology and template for feasibility and operational plans for regional hubs. Each partner filled in the template with information about its country and E-Zavod processed the information. E-Zavod, with the support of MCBO and the collaboration of PPs, worked also on structuring workshops to facilitate peer-to-peer (P2P) knowledge sharing, with the first workshop planned for September 2024. In order to meet the target and have more participation in the meetings, the first meeting was postponed to the second period. MCBO also helped the OCU launch activity A1.3, focusing on showcase hubs.
In WP2, led by ANATOLIKI, stakeholder engagement was key to understanding and implementing the 5-helix approach for the repair hubs. ANATOLIKI compiled contributions from eight countries into the Joint Needs Analysis (D 2.2.1).and developed guidelines on engaging stakeholders in collaboration with PP7 FODSA. As part of the process for creating the Joint Needs Analysis, interviews were conducted with 3-4 training experts from each participating country. Furthermore, ANATOLIKI and DECOP conducted analyses of existing repair programs and training needs. DECOP created templates for monitoring co-creation activities with stakeholders and engaged key brands to assess their needs. These efforts contributed to deliverable D 2.1.1, outlining stakeholder engagement guidelines.
In WP3, MCBO initiated the analysis of consumer behavior regarding repairs, which will lead to the creation of a transnational report on behaviors to change and a list of identified nudging strategies (Activity 3.1 REPper Nudge piloting). MCBO also collaborated with AVITEM on communication activities, creating a project website, and managed operational and financial coordination, organizing meetings and supporting partners with financial reporting. Throughout April 2024, key documents were finalized and uploaded, including templates for best practices and engagement activities.
From January to December 2024, the REPper project made significant progress in fostering a repair economy through transnational cooperation and local engagement. The project has successfully aligned its activities with its key objectives, delivering impactful outputs and engaging a wide network of stakeholders.
Project Coordination - The Metropolitan City of Bologna (MCBO), as LP, played a crucial role in coordinating project activities, ensuring smooth implementation, and overseeing key deliverables. The project started with the internal setup, including financial and administrative structuring, communication frameworks, and the launch of the project website. Initial meetings, both virtual and in Lisbon, facilitated alignment on guidelines, deliverables, and timelines. A major milestone was the development of feasibility and operational plans for the REPper Hubs and the regional assessment report, laying a strong foundation for the project.
Work Package 1 - At the project level, feasibility studies and operational plans were developed to provide clear direction for the establishment of these hubs, setting shared operational criteria and procedures. In parallel, a testing and assessment report was produced to evaluate the effectiveness of the adopted solutions, identify best practices, and highlight potential areas for improvement. Moreover, the partners collaborated on defining a common methodology for hub implementation, contributing to the creation of the O1.1 Joint Canvas document, which serves as a key tool for ensuring coherence and standardization across transnational activities. Two key workshops were organized to facilitate peer-learning (A 1.2): the first, held on 10th September 2024, focused on building bridges in repair communities to overcome barriers, and the second, held on 20th November 2024, addressed effective communication strategies. 
Work Package 2 - Under the leadership of ANATOLIKI, the project has significantly contributed to capacity-building efforts for repair professionals. Key deliverables include the D2.1.1 Guidelines on how to define, map and engage stakeholders, D2.2.1 Joint Needs Analysis, and D2.2.2 Skills Portfolio for repair trainers and business supporters. All PPs actively participated in stakeholder mapping and the development of training resources, ensuring the five-helix approach was integrated into the creation of repair hubs. These efforts have laid the groundwork for structured training programs aimed at strengthening repair enterprises across Europe.
Work Package 3 - The REPper project placed a strong emphasis on changing consumer behavior toward repair practices. MCBO led the REPper Nudge Piloting activity, analyzing behaviors and identifying effective nudging techniques to encourage repair habits. The drafting of D3.1.1 provided a comprehensive cross-country analysis of behaviors to be changed, offering valuable insights to refine and improve the project's nudging strategies.
Communication, Dissemination, and Stakeholder Engagement - Project partners actively engaged in communication and dissemination efforts to ensure visibility and stakeholder involvement. The project's communication activities included the development of a comprehensive communication strategy and the creation of detailed guidelines, tools and templates. Partners received customized support to meet their individual communication needs, while a dedicated project website was created to host content, articles and resources. A project video was produced by AViTeM with all partner countries to showcase the collective results, while close coordination with AE and OCU ensured the development of effective communication tools for the hub showcase and REPper Factory.
During the year, partners actively participated in coordination meetings, ensuring alignment with the Interreg Euro-Med governance framework. ANATOLIKI took a leading role in carbon footprint reporting, collecting and analyzing data to measure the project's environmental impact and suggest mitigation measures. These reports reinforced the project's commitment to sustainability.
The first year of the REPper project was marked by strong cooperation, the establishment of core elements for repair centers, the development of capacity building structures, and significant efforts to promote behavioral change. Through effective governance, strategic dissemination, and environmental awareness, the project has laid the foundation for further progress in strengthening the repair economy in Europe.
During this reporting period, the Metropolitan City of Bologna (MCBO), as Lead Partner, continued to provide guidance at European and local levels. Monthly online coordination meetings were organized with all partners, complemented by thematic sessions on communication (coordinated by AVITEM).
In April 2025, MCBO represented REPper at the Interreg Euro-MED Innovative Sustainable Economy Community of Practice meeting in Sarajevo, and engaged in working groups related to resource valorisation.
Together with AULEDA, MCBO co-organized the in-person partner meeting held in Vlorë, Albania, on June 3–4, 2025, where partners shared progress on work packages, deliverables, and next steps.
WP1 
Under the coordination of E-Zavod, partners advanced in the design and testing of the hubs’ network of pilot services, aimed at supporting new enterprises in the repair sector. Regular communication ensured alignment and timely delivery of outputs, while joint progress updates were supported by the continuous collection of hub characteristics, service testing results, and assessments. Knowledge exchange among partners and stakeholders was particularly valuable, especially the sharing of best practices and lessons learned across territories.
As part of the hub-related activities, the project’s workshop series continued with the third online seminar in May 2025, focusing on empowering consumers with data to evaluate when repair is preferable to replacement. Partners agreed to continue the cycle with a fourth seminar in November 2025. In addition, an inventory of actions and materials created within the hubs is being consolidated to showcase practical solutions and inspire replication.
WP2 
As Work Package 2 leader, ANATOLIKI coordinated the consortium’s efforts to strengthen capacities for the repair economy. A key milestone was the development of the Joint Training First-aid Kit (Deliverable 2.2.3), created by Altroconsumo with the support of ANATOLIKI and other partners. ANATOLIKI ensured alignment across the partnership by providing guidelines and templates, and by preparing the ground for Deliverable 2.2.4 on pilot trainings and the cross-regional training assessment.
Thanks to the support of DECO, partners updated and consolidated the list of key stakeholders, ensuring that local professionals, SMEs, start-ups, vocational training centres, and schools are properly engaged. This process contributes to embedding repair and circular economy concepts into local training circuits and curricula. A plan for local factory running and trainings was also established, paving the way for practical skill-building in the upcoming semester.
WP3
During this period, the REPper project advanced activities in behavioural change, aiming to promote repair practices among citizens. The Nudge Piloting activity was centrally coordinated by MCBO to ensure a coherent approach across all participating regions. A common methodology and template were developed and shared with partners to guide the design and local implementation of nudges. Pilot activities are scheduled to launch in summer 2025, targeting diverse audiences such as tourists, residents, and young people, with first results expected in Thessaloniki in October 2025.
The project started discussing during the Consortium Meeting activities to mobilize multi-sectoral and multi-level decision makers foreseen for the next semester, including the organisation of focus groups and roundtables to foster stakeholder engagement and policy support.

fr.name: The new REPair perspective
fr.description:
Pourquoi pas? il est plus urgent que jamais de repenser les modèles d'entreprise traditionnels, de redécouvrir la centralité de la valeur humaine dans la poursuite de l'innovation, et de revoir les comportements, le tout vers un modèle d'économie circulaire.
Pourquoi pas? pour soutenir les PME dans l'économie de la réparation, accroître les compétences en matière de réparation et poursuivre la transition vers des choix plus durables. Le projet REPper vise à relever ces défis et à proposer des solutions concrètes pour y répondre.
Un défi global tel que la durabilité, en réduisant les déchets, en augmentant la réutilisation, en adoptant des pratiques régénératives et - cœur du projet - en mettant en œuvre la réparation, agissant à une échelle transnationale.
Comment? A travers 3 domaines d'action axés sur la coopération transfrontalière: 

1. La mise en place d'un REPper HUBS NETWORK - un point de contact unique, physique et/ou numérique - pour concevoir et tester un réseau transnational de services pilotes. 

2. La création d'une REPper FACTORY pour la montée en compétence et la requalification, offrant une formation au plus haut niveau de l'UE, afin de réduire les déséquilibres entre les régions méditerranéennes. 

3. La construction d’une nouvelle culture de la réparation par le biais de "coups de pouces" - NUDGES - qui peuvent déclencher des changements positifs et de nouvelles habitudes. 
À cette fin, REPper réunit des partenaires hautement qualifiés et diversifiés, capables de capitaliser les résultats passés, de les mettre en œuvre avec de nouvelles actions pour des cibles publiques et privés: PME, autorités publiques, agences sectorielles, organisations de soutien aux entreprises, écoles et, enfin, le citoyenneté. 
Et si "Pourquoi pas?" est une question qui offre de multiples solutions pour parvenir à une Europe plus verte, REPper veut relever le défi en offrant une perspective différente.
